name: Coca
benefit: Altitude sickness, fatigue, digestion; traditional leaf chewing and tea across Andean Peru/Bolivia.
recipe: Mate de coca: 5–10 leaves in hot water.
sourcing: Legal in Peru, Bolivia, Argentina as traditional plant; restricted elsewhere.

name: Muña
benefit: Andean mint; digestive, antimicrobial, respiratory support.
recipe: Fresh leaves or dried infusion after meals.

name: Chanca Piedra
benefit: 'Stone breaker' — traditional Amazonian remedy for kidney/gallstones, liver support.
recipe: Decoction 200 ml 2× daily; or capsules 500 mg.

name: Cat's Claw (Uña de Gato)
benefit: Immune modulator, anti-inflammatory; studied in rheumatoid arthritis.
sourcing: Amazonian vine bark; sustainable sourcing essential.

name: Sangre de Grado
benefit: 'Dragon's blood' — red sap for wound healing, GI ulcers; FDA-approved crofelemer for diarrhea.
recipe: Few drops topically on wounds; or diluted in water internally.
attribution: Crofelemer (Mytesi) is FDA-approved for HIV-associated diarrhea

name: Camu Camu
benefit: Extremely vitamin-C-rich Amazonian berry; immune and mood support.

name: Lucuma
benefit: Nutrient-dense Andean fruit; low-glycemic sweetener, immune support.

name: Maca (red/black)
benefit: Hormone balance; red for women, black for men, yellow general. See main maca entry.

name: Purple Corn
benefit: Anthocyanin-rich; cardiovascular and blood-sugar support, traditional chicha morada drink.
recipe: Boil cobs with cinnamon, clove, pineapple rind — traditional chicha.

name: Quinoa
benefit: Complete-protein Andean grain; rebuilding and nourishing.
recipe: Rinsed and cooked like rice; 1 cup cooked per serving.

name: Kiwicha (Amaranth)
benefit: High-protein Andean grain; iron-rich, gluten-free.
recipe: Popped as snack or cooked porridge.

name: Cañihua
benefit: Cousin of quinoa, smaller seed, higher iron.

name: Yacón
benefit: Prebiotic FOS (fructooligosaccharide)-rich tuber; gut microbiome and blood-sugar support.
recipe: Fresh tuber like jicama; or yacón syrup 1 tsp.

name: Mashua
benefit: Andean tuber traditionally used to reduce libido (opposite of maca). Mild antimicrobial.
recipe: Cooked tuber; infused tea from tubers.

name: Oca
benefit: Andean tuber; nutritious food, gentle on digestion.

name: Ulluco
benefit: Andean tuber, mineral-rich.

name: Paico
benefit: 'Epazote' — traditional anti-parasitic and carminative.
recipe: Small amount cooked with beans; do not exceed traditional culinary amounts.
attribution: Essential oil (ascaridole) is toxic — culinary use only

name: Huacatay
benefit: Peruvian black mint; digestive, antibacterial, traditional culinary.
recipe: Fresh leaves in sauces; tea for digestion.

name: Chicha Morada
benefit: Traditional purple-corn beverage; antioxidant, cardiovascular support.

name: Emoliente
benefit: Classic Lima street drink with boiled barley, flaxseed, horsetail, alfalfa, and bitter herbs.
recipe: Hot from street vendor; home recipes vary.

name: Aguaymanto
benefit: Golden berry / Cape gooseberry; vitamin A/C-rich, traditional anti-inflammatory.

name: Achiote
benefit: Seed used for natural dye and topically for skin healing.

name: Pau d'arco
benefit: Amazonian bark; anti-fungal and anti-parasitic traditionally, immune adjunct.

name: Guayusa
benefit: Amazonian caffeinated leaf; sustained energy with L-theanine.
sourcing: Ecuadorian Amazon; Runa Tea largest exporter.

name: Yerba Mate
benefit: South American caffeinated holly; digestion, energy, antioxidants.
recipe: Traditional gourd with bombilla; 1–3 cups daily.
attribution: High-temp drinking linked to esophageal cancer risk

name: Palo Santo
benefit: Sacred incense wood; traditional smoke-cleansing.

name: Copaiba
benefit: Amazonian oleoresin; anti-inflammatory topically and internally.

name: Sacha Inchi
benefit: Amazonian peanut; omega-3-rich oil, cardiovascular support.
recipe: 1 tbsp oil daily; or roasted seeds.

name: Chuchuhuasi
benefit: Amazonian bark; anti-inflammatory and energizing tonic, traditional aphrodisiac.
recipe: Bark soaked in aguardiente (rum) as traditional tonic.

name: Clavo Huasca
benefit: Amazonian vine; traditional aphrodisiac, clove-like aroma.

name: Ayahuasca
benefit: Traditional sacred brew — requires ceremonial context. Contains DMT; illegal in most countries.

name: San Pedro (Huachuma)
benefit: Traditional Andean ceremonial cactus. Contains mescaline; controlled in most countries.

name: Ruda
benefit: Traditional 'cleansing' herb; used in folk rituals and mild digestive complaints.
recipe: Very small amounts only; toxic in excess.

name: Matico
benefit: Amazonian wound-healing and antimicrobial leaf.

name: Chuño
benefit: Traditional Andean preserved food; easy to digest, staple during altitude travel.

name: Molle
benefit: Peruvian pepper tree; fruit used as mild laxative, bark for wounds.
recipe: Traditional decoctions; seeds as pink-pepper seasoning.

name: Lepidium peruvianum
benefit: Variety of maca traditionally preferred for women.

name: Noni
benefit: Pacific/Amazonian fruit; traditional tonic and immune support.

name: Graviola (Soursop)
benefit: Amazonian fruit; traditional antimicrobial and calming.
recipe: Fresh fruit; tea from leaves (limit long-term — neurotoxicity concerns).
attribution: Chronic leaf use linked to atypical parkinsonism

name: Guanábana leaf
benefit: Duplicate of Graviola leaf.

name: Wiri wiri pepper
benefit: Andean/Amazonian chili; circulation and digestive heat.

name: Boldo
benefit: Chilean liver tonic and digestive bitter.
recipe: 1 tsp leaves as tea; short courses only.
attribution: Ascaridole content — not for long-term use

name: Hercampuri
benefit: Andean bitter; liver and cholesterol support.

name: Suelda con suelda
benefit: 'Bone-setter' — topical poultice for fractures and sprains.

name: Cola de caballo
benefit: Horsetail — diuretic, mineral-rich (silica), bone support.
recipe: 1 tsp dried in tea daily; short courses.

name: Llantén
benefit: Plantain leaf — topical wound healing, demulcent for coughs and throat.
recipe: Fresh leaf poultice; or leaf infusion.

name: Yerba luisa
benefit: Calming digestive tea; evening comfort.
recipe: 1 tsp leaves in hot water 5 min.

name: Manzanilla (Chamomile)
benefit: Calming, digestive, anti-inflammatory. Widely used in Spanish-speaking herbal tradition.

name: Anís estrellado
benefit: Star anise for infant colic, digestive upset.
recipe: Weak tea only for infants; stronger for adults.
sourcing: Asia; use Illicium verum — not Japanese star anise which is toxic.
attribution: Confirm species — Japanese star anise neurotoxic

name: Azahar
benefit: Orange blossom; calming for anxiety and sleep.
recipe: Tea from dried flowers; or water for baking.

name: Tilo
benefit: Linden flower — gentle sedative, for colds and anxiety.

name: Toronjil
benefit: Lemon balm — calming, antiviral (HSV), digestive.

name: Hierbabuena
benefit: Spearmint — digestive, anti-androgenic (useful in PCOS).
recipe: Fresh leaves in tea twice daily.

name: Guayaba (Guava)
benefit: Leaves for diarrhea; fruit vitamin C-rich.

name: Tamarindo
benefit: Mild laxative, cooling, rich in tartaric acid.

name: Nopal
benefit: Prickly pear pad; blood sugar and cholesterol support.

name: Piñón
benefit: Traditional topical for skin; seed oil also used. Seed highly toxic internally.
attribution: Seeds contain toxic curcin — external only

name: Malva
benefit: Mucilaginous soother; coughs, gut inflammation, topical on skin.
recipe: Leaf and flower tea; cooled compress topically.

name: Alfalfa
benefit: Mineral-rich tonic; vitamins K, C, trace minerals.
